observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the following:
a. Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation with the
Municipal Treasurer to determine the unreconciled balances so that
discrepancies can be immediately traced and necessary adjusting entries
be made accordingly;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the
books for valid reconciling items per books such as bank memos,
interest, taxes, errors and other unrecorded adjustments;
c. Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees or holder in
due course to surrender the stale checks for cancellation and issuance of
a new one; and
d. Municipal Accountant to record adjustments at year end for unreleased
checks and to restore stale checks to the Cash in Bank and payable
accounts.

2.12 We also recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to categorize
the PPE by asset class in the RPCPPE.

3.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal
Accounting Office copies of the report of completed projects with
necessary supporting documents;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the identified completed
projects to the appropriate PPE accounts and to record the related
accumulated depreciation and the corresponding depreciation expense;
c. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Accountant to determine the cost
of unissued materials for the 12 projects; and
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the cost of construction
materials to Construction Materials Inventory account prior its
issuance.

3.11 We also recommended and the Municipal Engineer and Accountant agreed
to coordinate and regularly reconcile their records to determine the
updated status and correct balances of the CIP accounts.

4.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Storekeeper/
Officer-In-Charge and Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the
Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively.

4.13 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Storekeeper
and Accounting Office to fully reconcile the PPE accounts recorded in the
books to establish the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts presented
in the financial statements;

4.14 We also recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare a complete
RPCPPE, and the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in the conduct and
completion of the physical inventory and refer to COA Circular No. 2020-
006 in the conduct of the next physical count of Property, Plant and
Equipment and the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the amount of RPT and SET receivables per books
based on the certified list of Real Property Taxpayers from the Treasurer’s
Office and make the necessary journal entries to adjust the receivable
accounts; and

5.10 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the
Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the
beginning of the year a duly certified list showing the names of the
taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which shall be
the basis of the latter to set-up the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivables
to conform with Sections 19 and 20, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for
LGUs.

6.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Reconcile the balance of the LDRRMF fund in the Special Trust Fund
and prepare the necessary adjusting or correcting entries to reflect the
accurate balances of relevant accounts; and
b. Revisit COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and
observe the proper accounting and recording of receipt and utilization of
LDRRMF.

7.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant maintain separate ledgers to record all
transfers of unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of
transfer;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate in
the monitoring and reconciling of the balance of the LDRRM Fund;
c. Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the LDRRM Fund
reflected in the General and Trust Fund books; and
d. MDRRMO to submit regularly to the Audit Team the monthly Report
on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF, duly prepared and certified by
the Municipal Accountant, reflecting the available funds, utilization and
balances of the yearly Special Trust Fund, which should reconcile with
the balance in the Notes to Financial Statements.

8.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant to perform the following:
a. Maintain general and subsidiary ledgers to record all transactions
pertaining to the Due to NGAs Account and to post monthly all the
JEVs drawn/prepared:
b. Verify, analyze and reconcile the balances between the RAO, JEV and
the Statement of Financial Position in order to present accurate
balances of the Due to NGAs account in the financial statements.

9.13 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare the financial reports on the operations
and financial condition of each local economic enterprise and for the
department heads to adopt the existing manual in the planning, budgeting,
implementation and reporting of the LEEs.

9.14 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor through the Sangguniang
Bayan agreed to conduct periodic review of the operations of LEEs and
evaluate the profitability and self-sufficiency of the individual LEEs.
Amendments of the Municipal Revenue Code involving collections of fees
by LEEs for a more viable and profitable operations may be considered,
notwithstanding the services provided therein.

10.11 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to henceforth,
investigate the circumstances and duration of the delay as soon as
notification was made by the contractor. In the event that the contract
time is extended, the guidelines provided in the Revised IRR of RA No.
9184 should be followed.

11.13 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, the members of the
Municipal Planning and Development Council, the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator, the Municipal Engineer and other
Municipal Officials concerned agreed to:
a) Ensure that priority development projects to be funded out of the 20%
Development Fund are well-planned and procurement-and
implementation-ready;
b) Specify the implementation dates and implement the priority
development projects programmed during the year as embodied in the
approved AIP; and
c) Conduct periodic review on the feasibility of the implementation of
PPAs and initiate appropriate measures to ensure optimum utilization
of the 20% Development Fund.

12.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the HRMO and all the department heads to review the existing
manpower complement, identify staffing gaps, and conduct a thorough
need assessment to ensure that the hiring and engagement of job order
personnel are in accordance with the operational and strategic needs of
the Municipality; and
b. Direct the HRMO Designate and other concerned personnel to prepare
an Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan, and formulate
policies and guidelines in hiring employees so that the necessity and
propriety of hiring job order employees are justified.

13.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Municipal Officials concerned and the GAD Focal Point System to prepare
the Municipality’s GAD Agenda for the three-year period as required
under Item C2 par. 1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and
Item 5 par. 6 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

14.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the GAD
Focal Point System to comply with JMC No. 2013-01 by regularly
monitoring the efficiency of the GAD plan and budget and check on the
actual expenditures in implementing gender-related programs, projects
and activities and to obtain the approval of the proper authority in case it
exceeds the approved budget.

15.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Treasurer to enforce collections of unpaid market rental fees
including surcharges thereof, otherwise impose the penal provisions as
provided in Municipal Ordinance No. 2011-007.

16.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
following:
a. Municipal Treasurer to regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant of
the report on market stall rentals that are due and collectible within the
year, as well as the surcharges and interests thereon, which should be
the basis of recording the receivable account at the beginning of the
accounting period; and
b. Municipal Accountant to henceforth prepare the necessary entries to
record the Receivable Account, in order to present fairly the financial
statements and to comply with the accrual basis of accounting as
prescribed under IPSAS 1 and MNGAS for LGUs Volume I.

16.9 We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to furnish a
copy of the total market stall collectibles from CY 2018 to CY 2024 to the
Municipal Accountant, so that necessary adjusting entries can be
prepared for the unrecorded receivables and income for those years.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to:
Conduct of investigation for PPEs which accountability could not be established despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, and to determine the last known location where the missing PPE items were installed, person accountable, circumstances of the loss and persons responsible for the loss. Once accountability/responsibility was pinpointed, demand shall be made to produce the item or pay the depreciated replacement cost; and
Request authority for derecognition from the books of accounts from the COA for PPEs whose accountability/responsibility could not be determined after investigation.
The Accounting Unit to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) reversing the PPEs derecognized totaling P12.711 million from the books in CY 2022 pending validation and verification of the nature of the PPEs missing and not found during physical count.
The GSO/MT to verify the history of non-existing PPEs such as prior disposal/transfers/donations or pending Request for Relief in accordance with Item 7.0 of the Circular.
We recommended, and the Management agreed to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the LGU by recording the costs of the structures and derecognize the same only when it is duly turned over to the DepEd supported by certificate of turn-over. Upon turn-over, ensure that JEV of derecognition is furnish to the school division concerned.
We recommended, and the Management agreed to reclassify the constructed fence in the Sanitary Landfill (Jamboree Site) at Barangay Mawal, Leon B. Postigo totaling P1.798 million to Other Land Improvements and its corresponding depreciation accordingly.
We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
Draw a JEV to correctly classify the road lot amounting to P0.502 million in the Road Networks account and derecognize the same from the Land account; and
Recognize the parcels of land amounting to P1.891 million supported by tax declaration from the Municipal Assessor.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to retrieve documents related to the payment of labor and draw a JEV to recognize the same as value of the related PPE and recompute for the accumulated depreciation in compliance with IPSAS 17.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
Make the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the payable accounts and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the JEV taking up the adjustments; and
Set up the liabilities in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria set out in IPSASs.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant to:
Record in the current year the leave benefits for employees and officials who are scheduled to retire and in their last term with the LGU, with a debit to Prior Period Adjustment and credit to Leave Benefits Payable amounting P0.601 million duly certified by the MBO and MT on availability of appropriation and cash back-up;
Ensure compliance with IPSAS 1 and IPSAS 20 by providing the required disclosures in the Notes to FS, specifically on the aggregate renumeration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those that are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and
Provide disclosure in the notes to FS on the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year.
b. The MHRMO to submit the List of Accumulated Leave Credits of Officials and Employee of the LGU as of December 31, 2024, and every quarter thereof, to the LFC thru the MBO for consideration in the appropriation for the succeeding years until the earned leave credits are backed up by appropriation and funds.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the remaining inventory of vaccines purchased in CY 2024, and make prior period adjustment since the books of accounts was already closed, and to henceforth record future receipts and issuance of the same using the perpetual inventory method; and
The Office of the Municipal Health furnish the Office of the Municipal Accountant copies of the inventory reports/PTRs, SSMI with RIS and Distribution List for of all donations in-kind received and issued by the Municipality.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to ensure that in CY 2025 submission of Financial Statements to include the following:
The SCBAA for which it is held publicly accountable and actual amounts, per IPSAS 24 guidelines and illustrative examples; and
The disclosure in the Notes to FS of the Reconciliation of the SCBAA and Statement of Financial Performance as per Section 3.20 of Annex G, COA Circular 2016-004

We recommended, and the Management agreed to disclose in the Notes to FS the following:
Nature, amounts of errors per line item of the FS affected, and other relevant information of the Prior Period Errors as required by Paragraph 54 of the IPSAS No. 3 to ensure reliability and relevance of the FS.
Implementation of the above-mentioned resolution along with EO 138, their details, financial, and non-financial effects to the LGU;
Revise the account name of the account “Share from IRA” to “Share from NTA” in compliance with the above-mentioned resolution and EO; and
Moving forward, to study constantly the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC) publications, COA accounting rules and regulations and consult with the audit team.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical count of PPE based on the PIP and continue the procedural guidelines in inventory taking provided in Section 6 of COA Circular 2020-06.
We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and MT to maintain and update PPELC and PCs per category of PPE and conduct periodic reconciliation of their records based on the guidelines provided in Section 6 of COA Circular 2020-006.
We recommended, and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee to tag the PPEs with property stickers and assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Item 5 of the Circular.

We recommended, and Management agreed with the following recommendations:
The LCE to constitute the Disposal Committee tasked to implement the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA-DBM JC No. 2024-1;
The Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of PPE and prepare the Report of Physical Count of PPE; and
The Property Division to prepare the IIRUP based on the RPCPPE, using the prescribed format for all PPE found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed, and submit to the Disposal Committee for appraisal and disposal.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to reconcile the balance of P1.678 million in the Cash in Bank – Local Currency Account for the TF – LDRRMF and the Trust Liability – DRRMF account in the TF books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account totaling P0.861 million to Machinery, P0.766 million and Military, Police and Security Equipment by P95,300.00; reclassify Furniture and Fixtures account to Military, Police and Security Equipment by P200,000.00, and adjust each depreciation accordingly.
Municipal Assessor to furnish copies of updated Tax Declarations and other related documents of parcels of land to the GSO to be used by the GSO in identifying the market value of land; and

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
 
Regularly update and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of real property taxpayers including all delinquent and current year tax due to support the RPT/SET Receivables recorded in the books; and
Review the delinquent RPT/SET Receivables and intensify its collection by notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law and conduct of information and education campaign on RPT payment. Also, consider recommending to the Sanggunian thru the LCE tax collection strategies, that would entice taxpayers to pay RPT and SET; and
Municipal Accountant to perform adjusting entries to recognize the delinquent RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts duly supported by individual taxpayers’ index card.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO/MT to reconcile their records and effect appropriate adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the Inventory accounts in the financial statements;
We recommended, and Management agreed to exert efforts in reconciling the balance in the Cash in Bank – Local Currency Account for the TF – LDRRMF and the Trust Liability – DRRMF account in the TF.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant:

Recognize the plants and trees aside from the land and to regularly update the records for the biological transformations of biological assets and see to it that agricultural produce is properly accounted for.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash for the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and MF – MOOE amounting to P2.724 million to STF bank account, TF-DRRM in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: P2.74 million unexpended/ obligated balance of the QRF and MF was transferred in the 1st quarter of 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant initiate the first procedures for disposition of dormant accounts in COA Circular 2023-008, after determining the existence of dormant accounts in the books including the following:

Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of all dormant accounts; and

Secure/collate all available documents relative to the said accounts to be reviewed and analyze to determine its existence, such as books of accounts, financial statements, ledgers, schedules and vouchers;

Inform, through the demand letters, those Accountable Officer who are still in service that failure to liquidate the cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his salary and the institution of other sanction as provided for under paragraphs 9.2 and 9.3 of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all disbursement of funds shall be covered by DVs and supporting documents prior to payments or transfer of funds.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:       

Consider the transfer of funds to the barangay thru a financial assistance be subject of liquidation upon completion of the purpose of the fund transfer. Ensure that transfer be supported by a memorandum of agreement and official receipt as proof of transfer;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the financial assistance as Due from LGUs to monitor its liquidation;

Moving forward, ensure that all financial assistance granted to barangays and lease contracts are duly approved by the Sanggunian thru appropriation ordinance in the annual budget or Sanggunian bayan resolution in the absence of the former.

We recommended, and Management agreed to evaluate the components included in the local development fund before these are approved to avoid charging expenditures that are not qualified to be funded therein such as administrative expenses which are proper charges to the GF.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Human Resource and Management Office (HRMO):

In coordination with the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of supporting documents for payments of monetization of leave credits as prescribed in the COA Circular 2012-001 as amended by COA Circular No. 2023-004.
Ensure that monetization of leave credits are applied first to available vacation leave credits before any sick leave credits in accordance with CSC Resolution No. 00034.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the concerned officials and employees to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the prompt submission of the financial reports with complete documentation as basis in establishing the validity and regularity of the payments made Non-submission without justifiable cause on the part of the officers and employees concerned on the required documents and financial report automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission as prescribed by law.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the concerned officials to comply with the existing rules and regulations in the GAA in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to promote effectiveness and efficiency in public service by ensuring adequate net pay of employees for their daily subsistence and ensure that net take home pay will be considered prior to the approval of loan applications.

Require the personnel who is assigned in the control of the fuel to prepare and submit timely and regularly the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for CY 2021 and onwards summarizing all the issuances for the month supported with the duly issued and approved Gas Slip together with the Delivery Receipt issued by the gasoline station upon withdrawal, for approval of the GSO/Treasurer and the Local Chief Executive to diligently monitor the issuance thereof; 

Likewise, the payment of fuel is to be attached with the usual documents coming from the BAC depending on the acquisition modality together with the Purchase Order, Certificate of Deposit issued by the Gasoline station and such other documentary evidences since asset method is used in the accounting of its fuel procurement and/or delivery while the consumption is accounted for as an expense upon withdrawal from the gasoline station.
observations.reason: Closed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management to submit legal basis to support the P7.1767 million disbursement why they will not be disallowed in audit which may results to the refund of the said financial assistance and other benefits granted to Job Order Employees and permanent officials and employees, respectively.

observations.recommendation: Use of the petty cash fund for payment for spare parts for the repair of vehicles is to be stopped. Follow the normal procurement processes required under R.A. 9184. The preparation of purchase request for the proposed procurement is to be based on the estimated needs of the entity taking into consideration the period of time the goods requested are available

Management direct the Accounting Office, to enforce strictly the control mechanism for the submission of the barangay financial transactions through the issuance of formal letters informing the Punong Barangays the immediate compliance of the duties and procedures specified in Item 4 – Specific Guidelines of COA Circular 2019-001 in order to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of barangay.
Management ensure that the accounting forms and reports were duly filled up, to provide stronger internal control of government operations, in accordance with the Volume II of the LGU-MNGAS.

observations.recommendation:
f. We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain complete PPELC and PCs per category of PPE, and conduct periodic reconciliation of their records.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:

g.	The GSO/Municipal Treasurer prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts; and

h.	Strictly observe the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances

We recommended and Management agreed to direct:

a.	The GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:

1.	Prepare and submit duly accomplished RPCI upon the conduct of physical count on inventories by the Inventory Committee; 

2.	Prepare and submit RSMI to the Accounting Office together with RIS and distribution list to support the recognition of expense in the books.

b.	The Accounting Office to:


1.	Record issuances of inventories as expense in the books only upon receipt of SSMI together with the RIS/Distribution List.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Issue demand letters to responsible officers and board of Directors of PARBFAMCO thru registered mail;

b.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary documents to support the request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005 and submit the same to the LCE for approval and adoption by the SB; and

c.	File with the COA the request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated fund transfer accounts amounting to P100,00.00 for the dropping of the same from the book of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in the verification/validation of the dormant and/or unnecessary bank accounts for the possible closing the same and/or reclassifying them to other existing bank accounts under the same fund and closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records; and 

b.	Close the old account and maintain only one depository account with LBP, ceasing the burden of transferring funds from one account to another for the same purpose

We recommended, and Management agreed to:

  programs and activities in the LDRRMFIP are feasible and done as planned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections to the authorized depository bank and to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the accounting records and cashbook balances every end of the month.

We strongly recommended that:
1. The Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee and direct them to conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality to facilitate the completion of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.
2. The Accounting Office maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish the correctness of the PPE accounts amounting to P195,332,950.93.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to adhere to pertinent sections of the RA 7160 to enforce payment of tax delinquencies.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board, in the preparation of Annual SEF Budget, prioritize projects, programs and activities pursuant to Item 4.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairman of the Board direct the Local School Board to maximize the use of Special Education Fund for the implementation of priority projects, programs and activities so that timely benefits derived therefrom can be availed by the beneficiaries.
We also recommend that the Local School Board adhere with the provisions of Department of Education (DepEd), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Department of the Interior and Local government (DILG) Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 specifically on the use of the SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 in the preparation of SEF budget.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Spearhead the provision of fund based on the required allocation of 1% of its IRA to strengthen LCPC’s programs, projects and activities and include the same in Annual Budget to be enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan of the municipality.
2. Direct the LCPC to enforce the formulation of Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan (prioritized according to needs for integration in the local development plan); Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC) and the Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC).
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collections of unpaid market rental fee plus interest thereof, otherwise impose proper sanctions relative thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer, Municipal SLP Focal Person and officials concerned to fully implement the Sustainable Livelihood Program so that intended beneficiaries can avail timely economic benefits that may derive therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all local officials concerned:
1. Fully utilize and timely implementation of projects funded from LGSF in the year it was granted to achieve the optimum benefits and purpose of which the fund was created.
2. Adequately plan so as to anticipate identifiable problems that will potentially delay projects implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and officials concerned to ensure that financial assistance extended to the barangays, National High School and individuals participating in the socio-cultural activities be supported by the above-mentioned documentary requirements to provide basis for monitoring the proper use of funds.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make sure that fund transfers be recorded in the Due from LGUs account instead of Subsidy so that the municipality can monitor the implementation of the proposed PPAs.
We also recommended that intended subsidies for private persons/employees be granted as a Cash Advance to a Disbursing Officer so that disbursements can monitored.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all officials concerned maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development so that optimum benefits derived therefrom can be availed by the constituents.
We also recommended that the concerned municipal officials enhance the monitoring and evaluation of the status and implementation of the 20% development fund in pursuance to Municipal’s mandate and faithful compliance of pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the transfer of unexpended balance to the special trust fund - MDRRMF immediately to facilitate funding of PPAs relative thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members of the MSWMB, the Municipal Treasurer and the MENRO to strictly collect garbage fees not only to business establishments but also to every household and fines/penalties be imposed to violators of the above mentioned ordinances.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the members of the MSWMB to prepare SWM plan to facilitate assessment and monitoring.

We recommended that expenditures incurred in violation of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2004-130 be refunded by the Liga ng mga Barangay President since this was considered “unnecessary and extravagant”.
We likewise recommended that henceforth the municipality refrain from incurring unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer properly plan and program the immediate implementation of the unimplemented projects to carry out the purpose for which these were granted otherwise, return of the funds to the Provincial Government and other SA will be required.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned Municipal Officials refrain from incurring additional PS expenses/cost until such time that the limitation is observed. The implementation of any increase in PS cost should be held in abeyance until the municipality shall not exceed its PS limitation/cap.
We also recommend that all Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the CY 2014 Productivity Enhancement Incentive at P32,170.00 per official/employee, or a total of P4,021,060.00 due to excess in personal services limitation as prescribed under Section 325 (a) of Republic Act No. 7160.
We also recommend that all Municipal Officials and Employees cause the refund of the CY 2015 Productivity enhancement Incentive equivalent to one month basic salary of employees or a total of P2,197,187.00 due to excess in personal services limitation as prescribed under Section 325 (a) of Republic Act No. 7160.

We further recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan immediately convene after the occurrence of major disasters in order that resolution declaring a state of calamity will be passed such that the utilization of QRF will be in accordance with regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer implement the priority projects of the Municipality strictly in accordance with the Individual Project Program and the Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates. Any deviation thereof, shall be reported in a Revised Individual Project Program and a Revised Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates detailing the date to be started and completed in order to monitor the status or percentage of completion of the project.
We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer undertake the installation of electrical works and foot walk of the school building at Barangay Gines, otherwise refund of the amount equivalent to the works not applied totaling P51,725.00 will be enforced. Also, submission of the POW charged to the funds of Congressman Gorriceta is required.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Engineer indicate in the Individual Project Program and the Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates the control number, date to be started and date to be completed such that the status or percentage of completion of the project could be monitored.
We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer conduct regular monitoring of priority projects being implemented by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality through the Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to occupy the position of LDRRM Officer and three (3) Staff following the position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6 Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to be able to properly perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer and Staff.
We recommend that the PMOs/end-users prepare PPMP and APP as required by RA 9184 to facilitate validation of the Accomplishment Report.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer properly plan and program the immediate implementation of the unimplemented projects to carry out the purpose for which it was granted otherwise, return the money to the Source Agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor work for the provision of a sufficient garage or motor pool where all of motor vehicles will be kept when not in use in order to prevent them from rapid deterioration.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor program the construction of a bodega for unserviceable properties and dispose those beyond repair according to the guidelines on Disposal.

We recommended that management cause the refund of the 2013 Productivity Enhancement Incentive at P33,284.00 per employee or a total of P4,310,278.00 due to excess in personal services limitation as prescribed under Section 325 (a) of Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommend that the municipality establish specific guidelines in the granting of AICS for medical, burial, fire and other financial assistance to the indigent residents.
In establishing the guidelines, we suggest that it should be patterned after the DSWD Administrative Order No. 5 series of 2008: “Omnibus Guideline on the Management of DSWD Operated Crisis Intervention Unit”.
We also recommend that in order to legally implement this assistance on the use of government funds appropriate resolution should be approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipality through the Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to occupy the position of LDRRM Officer and three (3) Staff following the position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to be able to properly perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer and Staff.

Recommendation:

     We recommend that  the  accountable  officers  turn  over  their
     collections daily to the liquidating officer as required under Section
     29 of NGAS, Volume I, to ensure the prevention  of  possible  risk  of
     loss and unauthorized use. Also,  require  the  Accounting  Office  to
     provide subsidiary ledger  for  each  accountable  officer  to  ensure
     validation of balances in the general  ledger  and  reconciliation  of
     accountability can be made immediately.


          Further,  we  recommend  that  the  Office  of   the   Municipal
     Accountant in coordination with the Office of the Municipal  Treasurer
     to monitor regularly  all  accountable  officers  to  adhere  existing
     policies on proper cash remittances of the Municipality.

Recommendation:

     We recommend that the municipality demand immediate  liquidation
     of overdue  unliquidated  cash  advances  and  enforce  measures  like
     immediate withholding of salaries of concerned personnel.  Discontinue
     the granting additional cash advance to official and  employee  unless
     previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting  thereof
     is made.

Recommendation:

     We reiterate that management (a) require the Treasurer’s  Office
     to update the AREs and maintain a complete file thereof;  (b)  require
     the Accounting Office to maintain  Equipment  Ledger  Cards/subsidiary
     ledger  cards;  (c)  investigate  and  make  appropriate  actions   on
     discrepancies, if any.

Recommendation:
          We recommend that  the  Municipality  intensify  its  collection
     capability to ensure achievement of its  targeted  revenue.  Moreover,
     revise its estimated revenue based on the past years’ actual  data  in
     order to establish estimated revenue that is realizable.

Recommendation:

     We recommend the Municipality to monitor the timely and  optimal
     utilization of the  20%  development  fund  and  cause  the  judicious
     implementation of development programs and projects with the end  view
     of complying with the authoritative issuances appurtenant thereto  and
     the attainment of expected desirable outcomes for the benefits of  its
     constituents.

Recommendation:

     We recommend that the LGU remit promptly and intact the share of
     the provincial government to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office monthly
     within fifteen (15) days  of  the  succeeding  month  as  required  in
     Section 9 of Republic Act (RA) No. 5447,  for  timely  utilization  of
     fund to effectively serves it purpose.

Recommendation:

     We  recommend  the  Local  School  Board  with   the   Municipal
     Government  to  monitor  the  timely  and  optimal   utilization   and
     implementation of the SEF for the necessary programs and projects  for
     the attainment of expected desirable outcomes for the benefits of  the
     students.

Recommendation:
          We  recommend   that   the   Municipality   submit   a   written
     justification as to why the  project  was  not  completed  within  the
     contract time duly supported with variation  orders  and  contractor’s
     notice.
          Also, we recommend that the Office  of  the  Municipal  Engineer
     monitor regularly all  projects  implemented  by  the  LGU  to  ensure
     projects are completed without unexpected losses to the municipality.

Recommendation:

     We recommend that the Management transfer  only  the  unexpended
     balance of QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE excluding those  intended  for  capital
     outlay, in the special trust account  of  the  Trust  Fund  Books  and
     ensure the inclusion of the necessary notes to financial statement  of
     the Trust Fund-LDRRMF composition and status as per COA Circular 2012-
     002.

Recommendation:

     We recommend the Municipality cautiously monitor the timely  and
     optimal  utilization  of  the   LDRRMF   and   cause   the   judicious
     implementation of necessary pre-disaster preparedness  and  mitigation
     programs, projects and activities with the end view of complying  with
     the authoritative issuances appurtenant thereto and the attainment  of
     expected desirable outcomes for the benefits of its constituents.

Recommendation:
          We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board together with
     the Municipal Information Officer  conduct  an  intensive  information
     campaign on  its  solid  waste  management  plan  with  emphasizes  on
     mandatory  segregation  at   source   form   households,   commercial,
     institutional, market areas, industrial sources, transport  facilities
     and utilities, agricultural areas and construction business, reminding
     the corresponding penalties for non-compliance.


          Also, LGU should revisit and re-enforce the Municipal  Ordinance
     No. 05-012 on Solid Waste Management System  to  be  imposed  strictly
     with constant monitoring.

Recommendation:

     We  recommend  the  Municipality monitor timely  and   optimal
     utilization of the 1% Local Council for the Protection of Children
     (LCPC) and cause the judicious implementation of programs and projects
     intended under the fund to  ensure  the  attainment  of  the  expected
     desirable outcomes for the benefit of its constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the following Municipal Departments Heads to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:

A.  The Municipal Accountant
1.	Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
2.	Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructure for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
3.	Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and 
4.	Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
B.  The Municipal General Services Officer
       1.	Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and 
       2.	Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
C.  The Municipal Engineer
       1.	Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: > Total amount of prior years’ assets including road networks has been transferred to the Registry of Public Infrastructure.                  > Roads and road components has been kept and maintained in each subsidiary ledger.
observations.reason: Need to ascertain more details like distribution costs and other depreciated replacement cost of all infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management upon planning should only consider programs/projects/activities that are qualified under DBM-DILG JMC No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011, to provide the appropriate, viable, and sustainable development programs and projects intended thereon.
observations.managementAction: Ordered all the officers concerned to strictly comply with the provision of the 20% Development Funds in the preparation of the annual budget of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Some projects have already been implemented but for the succeeding budget we commit to strictly complied with the guidelines set by authorities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately effect necessary adjustments to correct the excessive contributions made by the municipality in its employer’s share to all employees monthly Pag-IBIG premium contributions in accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9679.
observations.managementAction: Most employees opt to still contribute 2% of their monthly salary as its counterpart; rest assured to immediately effect adjustments to correct the excessive LGU counterpart Pag-ibig premium contributions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management refrain from charging the regular expenses to the Special Education Fund. Instead, allocate or use such fund for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition of books and periodicals; and sports development which cannot be charged against the downloaded MOOE of the schools. Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth by the provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: Ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of resources for priority programs and projects which would complement, as well as reinforce budgeting priorities at the different levels of decision making.
observations.reason: In preparation of SEF Budget, we follow the Policies & Guidelines contained in JCs of DepEd, DBM and DILG (JC#1, s.1998, JC 1-A s. 2000, JC # 1-B s. 2001 and JC#4, 2001 on the use and purpose of the SEF as provided under RA#7160 and its IRR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the municipality fast track the implementation of Bottom-up Budgeting (BuB) projects to ensure timely attainment of the program’s objectives.
Also, we recommend that Municipality thru the BuB Focal Person coordinate regularly with the participating national government agencies to ensure funds of BuB projects and other programs are released on time.
observations.managementAction: fast track implementation of BuB Projects

Inactive ordinance which defeated the purpose of its enactment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality through the Sangguniang Bayan hire qualified personnel to occupy the position of LDRRM Officer and three (3) Staff following the position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6 Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to be able to properly perform their functions and responsibilities as LDRRM Officer and Staff.

We recommend that the LDRRM Officer - Designate religiously observe the timely submission of the annual LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF (Annex B) to the COA Auditor copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned department heads to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on each findings and recommendations, as presented in Annex III, and discussed in detail in Part II-Detailed Findings and Recommendations portion of the CY 2014 Annual Audit Report, in compliance with Section 88 of the General Provisions of RA No. 10633, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2014.

Recommendation:

           We reiterate that the municipality demand immediate  liquidation
      of overdue  unliquidated  cash  advances  and  enforce  measures  like
      immediate withholding of salaries of concerned personnel.  Discontinue
      the granting additional cash advance to official and  employee  unless
      previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting  thereof
      is made.

Recommendation:

           We recommended that management (a) create an inventory committee
      to conduct  complete  inventory  count  of  all  property,  plant  and
      equipment at least once a year  in  accordance  with  regulations  and
      reconcile with the balances per accounting records;  (b)  require  the
      Treasurer’s Office to update the AREs and  maintain  a  complete  file
      thereof; (c) require  the  Accounting  Office  to  maintain  Equipment
      Ledger  Cards/subsidiary  ledger  cards;  (d)  investigate  and   make
      appropriate actions on discrepancies, if any.

Recommendation:

           We recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  following
      Municipal Departments Heads to comply with  the  requirements  of  COA
      Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:



           A.  The Municipal Accountant

               1. Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record  the  beginning
                 balance of the local road network and its components in the
                 general ledger and  the  Local  Road  Network  Ledger  Card
                 (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;

               2. Support the JV with the Registry of Public  Infrastructure
                 for Roads, working paper on the distribution of  costs  for
                 the road components, and working paper on the determination
                 of the depreciated replacement  cost  for  road  components
                 with no available cost per registry;

               3. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for  roads  and  road
                 components for every road network; and

               4. Prepare a lapsing schedule  for  the  computation  of  the
                 depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

           B.  The Municipal General Services Officer

                  1.   Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

                  2.   Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for
                 all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)

           C.  The Municipal Engineer

                  1.   Provide the Municipal Accountant and  the  Municipal
                 General Services Officer with the complete description  and
                 cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Recommendation:

           We recommend that  the  Management  upon  planning  should  only
      consider programs/projects/activities that are  qualified  under  DBM-
      DILG JMC No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011, to provide the appropriate,
      viable, and sustainable development  programs  and  projects  intended
      thereon.

Recommendation:

           We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  immediately  effect
      necessary adjustments to correct the excessive contributions  made  by
      the municipality in its employer’s share to all employees monthly Pag-
      IBIG premium contributions in accordance with  Section  7  of  RA  No.
      9679.

Recommendation:

           We recommend that  the  management  refrain  from  charging  the
      regular expenses to the Special Education Fund. Instead,  allocate  or
      use such fund for the construction and  repair  of  school  buildings;
      facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition  of  books
      and periodicals;  and  sports  development  which  cannot  be  charged
      against the downloaded MOOE of  the  schools.  Also,  the  LSB  should
      evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included  in  the  SEF
      budget, taking into consideration the  objectives  set  forth  by  the
      provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.

Recommendation:
           We recommend that the municipality fast track the implementation
      of Bottom-up Budgeting (BuB) projects to ensure timely  attainment  of
      the program’s objectives.
           Also, we recommend that Municipality thru the BuB  Focal  Person
      coordinate  regularly  with  the  participating  national   government
      agencies to ensure funds  of  BuB  projects  and  other  programs  are
      released on time.

Recommendation:

           We reiterate that  the  municipality  implement  the  Ecological
      Solid  Waste  Management  system  such  as  segregation   at   source,
      segregated  collection  and  establishment   of   Materials   Recovery
      Facility/s, recycling and composting method in all barangays under its
      jurisdiction.

Recommendations

  We recommended that the inventory committee religiously conduct  physical
  count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the  Municipality
  to facilitate the completion of inventory of PPE and prepare  the  Report
  on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.

  We likewise, recommend that the Accounting Office maintain the prescribed
  PPELC which should  be  reconciled  with  the  RPCPPE  to  establish  the
  correctness of the PPE accounts amounting to P172,725,859.61.


  Further, we recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  should  require  the
  Municipal  Accountant  to  provide  the   corresponding   allowance   for
  depreciation.

  The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management  thru
  Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-06 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendation

documents such as proof that savings were  generated  through  cost  cutting
measures undertaken by LGU to determine validity of the claim.

The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-03 dated February 10, 2017.

Recommendation


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant
to submit the above-mentioned documents to facilitate further review of  the
concerned transactions.

The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to  management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-10 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant  and
Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount of  P946,089.69  to  the
Bureau of Internal Revenue.  Henceforth, taxes  withheld  from  payments  to
suppliers and salaries of employees be remitted promptly  and  regularly  in
accordance with the provisions of BIR Revenue Regulation No. 16-2005.


The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to  management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-09 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendations

We recommended that expenditures incurred in violation of COA  Circular  No.
2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG Memorandum  Circular No. 2004-130
be refunded by the Liga ng mga Barangay President since this was  considered
“unnecessary and extravagant”.


We likewise  recommended  that  henceforth  the  municipality  refrain  from
incurring unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures in the future.

The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to  management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No.2017-11 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer - Designate  religiously  observe  the
timely submission of the annual LDRRMFIP (Annex A) and  the  monthly  Report
on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF (Annex  B)  to  the  COA  Auditor  copy
furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO.

The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to  management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-07 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendations

We strongly recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  GAD  Focal
Point System to carefully study PCW, DILG, DBM  and  NEDA  Joint  Memorandum
Circular  No.  2013-01  and  follow  the  procedural   guidelines   in   the
formulation GAD Accomplishment Report.

We  further  recommended  that  the  Sanggunian  Bayan  pass  an   ordinance
concerning the adoption of a Gender and  Development  Code  to  enforce  the
formulation of GAD Accomplishment Report.

The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to  management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-08 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendation

We  recommended  that  the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  instruct  the  BAC
Secretariat to post all procurement in  the  Portal  as  prescribed  in  the
above-mentioned laws, rules and  regulations  to  promote  transparency  and
achieve efficiency in the procurement process of the municipality.

The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to  management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-02 dated January 25, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the
corresponding allowance for depreciation of  the  properties  based  on  the
formula as provided for by existing rules and regulations.


We recommended that the  Municipal  Treasurer  immediately  return  or
deposit the remaining unspent balances of the DAP/PDAF funds to  the  Bureau
of Treasury amounting P619,346.11 and to the Source Agencies for other  fund
transfers totaling P2,562,588.44.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  together  with  the  Planning
and  Development Officer and Municipal Engineer  properly  plan  and  program
the immediate implementation of the unimplemented projects to carry out  the
purpose for which these were granted otherwise, return of the funds  to  the
Provincial Government and other SA will be required.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  and  concerned  Municipal
Officials refrain from incurring  additional  PS  expenses/cost  until  such
time that the limitation is observed.  The implementation  of  any  increase
in PS cost should be held in  abeyance  until  the  municipality  shall  not
exceed its PS limitation/cap.


We also recommended that all Municipal Officials and  Employees  cause
the refund of the CY 2014 Productivity  Enhancement  Incentiveat  P32,170.00
per official/employee or a total of P4,021,060.00 due to excess in  personal
services limitation as prescribed under Section 325 (a) of Republic Act  No.
7160.


We also recommended that all Municipal Officials and  Employees  cause
the refund of the CY 2015 Productivity enhancement Incentive  equivalent  to
one month basic salary of employees or  a  total  of  P2,197,187.00  due  to
excess in personal services limitation as prescribed under Section  325  (a)
of Republic Act No. 7160.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  discontinue  the  payment  of
honoraria of LSB and Special  Education teachers and other expenses  such  as
utilities and administrative supplies from the Special Education Fund.


We further recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan immediately convene  after
the occurrence of major disasters  in  order  that  resolution  declaring  a
state of calamity will be passed such that the utilization of  QRF  will  be
in accordance with regulations.


We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste  Management  Board  prepare
budget proposals for the development of Sanitary  Landfill and  see  to  it
that PPAs relative be provided with  a  budget  and  included  in  the
succeeding year’s annual budget for proper implementation.


We recommended that the Municipality  through  the  Sangguniang  Bayan
hire qualified personnel to occupy the position of LDRRM Officer  and  three
(3) staff following the position titles and salary grades  set  by  Rule  6,
Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121 and Item 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of NDRRMC,  DILG,
DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014  to  be
able to properly  perform  their  functions  an  responsibilities  as  LDRRM
Officer and Staff.


We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit at the beginning  of
the  year a  list  of  all  ongoing  government  programs/projects/activities
(PPAs) of the Municipality and those that are to be implemented  during  the
year.


We recommended  that  the  PMOs/end-users  prepare  PPMP  and  APP  as
required by RA 9184 to facilitate validation of the Accomplishment Report.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together  with  the  Municipal
Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer  properly  plan  and
program the immediate implementation of the unimplemented projects to  carry
out the purpose for which it was granted otherwise, return the money to  the
Source Agencies.


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor work for the  provision  of  a
sufficient garage or motor pool where all of motor  vehicles  will  be  kept
when not in use in order to prevent them from rapid deterioration.


We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor  program  the  construction
of a bodega for unserviceable properties and  dispose  those  beyond  repair
according to the guidelines on Disposal.


We recommended that the municipality establish specific guidelines  in
the  granting  of  AICS  for  medical,  burial,  fire  and  other  financial
assistance to the indigent residents.  In establishing  the  guidelines,  we
suggest that it should be patterned after the DSWD Administrative Order  No.
5. Series of 2008; “Omnibus Guideline on the  Management  of  DSWD  Operated
Crisis Intervention Unit.”


We  also  recommended  that  in  order  to  legally  implement   this
assistance on the use of government funds appropriate resolution  should  be
approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  concerned  department
heads  to  submit  the  duly  accomplished  AAPSI  on  each   findings   and
recommendations, as presented in Part III, and discussed in detail  in  Part
II-Detailed Findings and Recommendations  portion  of  the  CY  2015  Annual
Audit Report, in compliance with Section 93 of the General Provisions of  RA
No. 10651, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2015.

The aforementioned audit observation was  communicated  to  management  thru
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-01 (16) dated January3, 2017.

We recommend that LEMEA President submit the internal guidelines to be followed in the grant of the CNA Incentive pursuant to item 5.1.3, such as: i) the specific criteria for determining who are entitled, and Ii) the distribution of the amount available and the rate of the CNA Incentive in accordance with item 4.2.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer submit the Accomplishment Report on targeted PPAs approved in the Annual Budget for CY 2014 and compliance of Department Heads and Employees on unnumbered Memorandum dated January 14, 2014 for evaluation on the legality and propriety of the grant of CNA Incentive for CY 2014.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer shall henceforth see to it that the required supporting documents are already attached before payment of CNA Incentive pursuant to Budget Circular No. 2014-2, dated December 2, 2014.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator stop the practice of charging the expenditure items not covered against the 20% Development Fund pursuant to Item 4.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor discontinue the payment of honoraria of LSB and Special Education teachers and other expenses such as utilities and administrative supplies from the Special Education Fund.

We also recommend that the Chairman, Municipal Local School Board (LSB) prioritize in the budget preparation the expenses chargeable to Special Education Fund in accordance with the above-cited implementing, laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that the LDRRM Officer - Designate stop the practice of charging to the Quick Response Fund without the necessary Sangguniang Bayan Resolution declaring state of calamity upon recommendation of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Councils (LDRRMC), otherwise future similar charges will be disallowed in audit.

We further recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan immediately convene after the occurrence of major disasters in order that resolution declaring a state of calamity will be passed such that the utilization of QRF will be in accordance with regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer implement the priority projects of the Municipality strictly in accordance with the Individual Project Program and the Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates. Any deviation thereof, shall be reported in a Revised Individual Project Program and a Revised Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates detailing the date to be started and date to be completed in order to monitor the status or percentage of completion of the project.

We further recommend that the Municipal Engineer undertake the installation of electrical works and foot walk of the school building at Barangay Gines, otherwise refund of the amount equivalent to the works not applied totaling P51,725.00 will be enforced. Also, submission of the POW charged to the funds of Congressman Gorriceta is required.

We likewise recommend that the Municipal Engineer indicate in the Individual Project Program and the Detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates the control number, date to be started and date to be completed such that the status or percentage of completion of the project could be monitored.

We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer conduct regular monitoring of priority projects being implemented by the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board prepare budget proposals for the development of Sanitary Landfill and see to it that PPAs relative thereto be provided with a budget and included in the succeeding year’s annual budget for proper implementation.

We  also  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Solid  Waste  Management  Board include in the budget the improvement of facilities such as foul odor elimination and acquisition of equipments for its MRF like plastic shredder and bio-reactor.

We recommend that the Municipal Health Officer stop the practice of receiving collections for MCP and TB DOTS and turn over the remaining Official Receipts to the Municipal Treasurer for proper recording and accounting of the Trust Fund. Also, disbursements from the fund shall pass through the proper disbursement system.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant require the submission of all documents relative to the collections and disbursements of the Philhealth MCP and TB DOTS Program.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer post a notification to the public and other forms of announcement and/or publicity for or otherwise relating to the PPA being implemented by the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer properly plan and program the immediate implementation of the unimplemented projects to carry out the purpose for which it was granted otherwise, return the money to the Source Agencies.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point System to carefully study PCW, DILG, DBM and, NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and follow the procedural guidelines in the formulation of the GAD Plan and Budget as well as the schedule prescribed in Section C.3 and C.5 of the Joint Circular.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor work for the provision of a sufficient garage or motor pool where all of motor vehicles will be kept when not in use in order to prevent them from rapid deterioration.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor program the construction of a bodega for unserviceable properties and dispose those beyond repair according to the guidelines on Disposal.

We recommend that management cause the refund of the 2013 Productivity Enhancement Incentive at P33,284.00 per employee or a total of P4,310,278.00 due to excess in personal services limitation as prescribed under Section 325 (a) of Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommend that the municipality establish specific guidelines in the granting of AICS for medical, burial, fire and other financial assistance to the indigent residents.
